title: Dashboard Page (app/dashboard/page.tsx)
content:
This file defines the main dashboard page for the DocMed application. ## Purpose The dashboard provides users with an overview of their DocMed workspace, including statistics on connected repositories, handled commits, and configured pairs. It also displays a log of recent commit activity. ## Key Components and Logic - **`DashboardPage` Component:** This is an asynchronous React Server Component responsible for fetching data and rendering the dashboard UI. - **Authentication:** It uses `@clerk/nextjs/server` to get the `userId` for personalized data retrieval. - **Data Fetching:** It concurrently fetches: - `listRepoPairs(userId)`: A list of configured repository pairs. - `listCommitLog(userId, 50)`: The 50 most recent commit logs. - `getCommitCount(userId)`: The total number of commits handled. - **Statistics Calculation:** It calculates `reposConnected` by filtering `pairs` that have both a `targetRepo.repoFullName` and `docRepo.repoFullName` defined. - **UI Structure:** The page is divided into: - A header with the page title and description. - A grid of `StatCard` components displaying key metrics. - A section for 'Recent Activity' which shows a list of commits or a message if no commits are found. - **`StatCard` Component:** A reusable presentational component that displays an icon, a numerical value, and a label for a statistic. ## Usage Example When a logged-in user navigates to the `/dashboard` route, this component will be rendered. It dynamically displays their connected repositories, commit statistics, and recent commit messages, providing a quick summary of their DocMed activity. ```typescript // Example of how the DashboardPage is rendered within the Next.js application // (This is illustrative and not directly from the provided code) // In app/layout.tsx or similar...
{/* The dashboard page content will be rendered here */}
```
